theme: one keyboard for three quarrelling languages
title: One Keyboard, Three Quarrelling Languages: Envelope-Optimal Keyslot Assignment
strategies: identity assignment: symbol i -> slot i (reproduces the checker's own baseline, ~0.1)

pooled-frequency centrality greedy: sum raw digraph counts across all languages, rank symbols by pooled demand, rank slots by centrality, match highest-to-cheapest -- dominated by whichever language has the largest absolute corpus, starves the others

consensus/conflict decomposition: normalize each language's digraphs to its own probability mass, extract the elementwise-min consensus core and place it on the cheapest slots first, then place the remaining periphery symbols by the MAX (not sum) of per-language idiosyncratic weight so the single most-affected language is protected on each contested slot
further headroom: true joint optimization (e.g. local search / simulated annealing over the full envelope objective) beyond the two-phase heuristic
